---
title: "Receptor Scan Summary Heatmaps"
---

# Summary
Generates a lower-triangular heatmap placing each cell type at its optimal receptor pair position for a chosen metric. Visualizes which receptor pairs are most discriminating for each cell type and reveals overlap in optimal pairs across cell types. Supports both selectivity and KL/EMD scan results; scan type is detected automatically from the accompanying YAML file.

# Imports
- Receptor scan result loading functions (`load_selec_scan_results`, `load_KL_EMD_scan_results`)
- Visualization libraries (matplotlib, seaborn, numpy)

# Parameters
- `scan_data_path`: Path to the scan CSV file; a matching `.yaml` must exist in the same directory
- `KL_METRIC`: For KL/EMD scans, selects the metric to display — `"KL_Divergence"` or `"EMD"`

# Outputs
- **Heatmap**: Each cell type placed at its optimal receptor pair position, colored by the selected metric value

```{python}
%config InlineBackend.figure_formats = ['svg']
import os

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import yaml

from figures.scanning_analyses.scan_runners import (
    load_KL_EMD_scan_results,
    load_selec_scan_results,
)

scan_data_path = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260520_selec_scan_full_filt.csv"
# For KL_EMD scans: "KL_Divergence" or "EMD". Does nothing for selectivity scans.
KL_METRIC = "KL_Divergence"

yaml_path = os.path.splitext(scan_data_path)[0] + ".yaml"
with open(yaml_path) as f:
    scan_type = yaml.safe_load(f)["scan_type"]

if scan_type == "selectivity":
    opt_selec, _, _, receptors, cell_types = load_selec_scan_results(scan_data_path)
    metric_array = opt_selec
    metric_label = "Selectivity"
elif scan_type == "KL_EMD":
    KL_results, EMD_results, receptors, cell_types = load_KL_EMD_scan_results(
        scan_data_path
    )
    metric_array = KL_results if KL_METRIC == "KL_Divergence" else EMD_results
    metric_label = KL_METRIC.replace("_", " ")
else:
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown scan_type: {scan_type}")

optimal_pairs = {}
involved_receptors = set()
for i, cell_type in enumerate(cell_types):
    matrix = metric_array[:, :, i]
    max_idx = np.unravel_index(np.nanargmax(matrix), matrix.shape)
    rec1_idx, rec2_idx = max_idx
    max_val = matrix[rec1_idx, rec2_idx]
    optimal_pairs[cell_type] = (receptors[rec1_idx], receptors[rec2_idx], max_val)
    involved_receptors.add(receptors[rec1_idx])
    involved_receptors.add(receptors[rec2_idx])

filtered_receptors = sorted(involved_receptors)
N_RECEPTORS = len(filtered_receptors)
receptor_to_idx = {rec: i for i, rec in enumerate(filtered_receptors)}

summary_matrix = np.full((N_RECEPTORS, N_RECEPTORS), np.nan)
position_to_cell_types = {}
for cell_type, (rec1, rec2, max_val) in optimal_pairs.items():
    i = receptor_to_idx[rec1]
    j = receptor_to_idx[rec2]
    i_lower, j_lower = (i, j) if i >= j else (j, i)
    if (i_lower, j_lower) not in position_to_cell_types:
        position_to_cell_types[(i_lower, j_lower)] = []
        summary_matrix[i_lower, j_lower] = max_val
    position_to_cell_types[(i_lower, j_lower)].append(cell_type)

mask = np.triu(np.ones_like(summary_matrix, dtype=bool), k=1)
summary_matrix[mask] = np.nan

vmin_val = np.nanmin(summary_matrix)
vmax_val = np.nanmax(summary_matrix)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))
sns.heatmap(
    summary_matrix,
    cmap="Reds",
    xticklabels=filtered_receptors,
    yticklabels=filtered_receptors,
    square=True,
    annot=False,
    ax=ax,
    cbar=False,
    vmin=vmin_val,
    vmax=vmax_val,
    linewidths=4.0,
    linecolor="lightgray",
    mask=False,
)
for i in range(N_RECEPTORS):
    for j in range(i + 1, N_RECEPTORS):
        ax.add_patch(
            plt.Rectangle((j, i), 1, 1, fill=True, color="lightgray", zorder=3)
        )
for (i, j), cts in position_to_cell_types.items():
    label = "\n".join(cts)
    ax.text(
        j + 0.5, i + 0.5, label, ha="center", va="center", fontsize=10, color="black"
    )
ax.set_title(
    f"Cell Types at Their Optimal Receptor Pair Positions\n(Color = {metric_label} Value)",
    fontsize=14,
    pad=20,
)
ax.set_xlabel("Receptor 2", fontsize=18)
ax.set_ylabel("Receptor 1", fontsize=18)
ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=90, labelsize=20, width=2, length=6)
ax.tick_params(axis="y", rotation=0, labelsize=20, width=2, length=6)
plt.tight_layout()

pos = ax.get_position()
cbar_ax = fig.add_axes(
    [pos.x1 + pos.width * 0.03, pos.y0, pos.width * 0.05, pos.height]
)
norm = plt.Normalize(vmin=vmin_val, vmax=vmax_val)
sm = plt.cm.ScalarMappable(cmap="Reds", norm=norm)
sm.set_array([])
cbar = fig.colorbar(sm, cax=cbar_ax)
tick_values = np.linspace(vmin_val, vmax_val, num=6)
cbar.set_ticks(tick_values)
cbar.set_ticklabels([f"{val:.2f}" for val in tick_values])
cbar.ax.tick_params(labelsize=20, width=2, length=6)
cbar.set_label(f"Optimal {metric_label}", fontsize=18)
plt.show()
```
